recommended_actions.description: Conduct a soil test to determine nutrient levels and pH, then amend the soil based on results.
Optimize fertilizer application according to wheat nutrient requirements and soil conditions.
Use soil moisture and weather forecasts to prevent crop stress and avoid waterlogging.

agronomic_references: Wheat performs well under moderate rainfall and balanced soil moisture.
Sandy soil loses nutrients quickly and benefits from smaller, frequent inputs.
Medium yield can improve through precision fertilization and irrigation scheduling.
safety_disclaimer: Consult a qualified agronomist before applying recommendations to a real farm.
